jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's characteristic opening sequence mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation and scream
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment that defines Homer's bumbling nature. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Stupid heater! Don't make me come in there.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with a malfunctioning heater
jokes.punchline: Threatening an inanimate object as if it could hear and respond
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's tendency to personify objects. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: You know me, Marge. I crave the hustle and bustle.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's procrastination on Christmas shopping
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming he enjoys crowds when clearly he's just lazy
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based irony showing Homer's self-deception about his motivations.

jokes.text: Oh, Santa's got a little trick up his sleeve.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about good toys being gone
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced confidence in his shopping abilities
jokes.explanation: Standard character moment showing Homer's unfounded optimism.

jokes.text: Sorry, these are for display only. This has been recalled. We're out of these. Register 9 is now closed.
jokes.setup: Homer's confidence about finding good toys
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire series of shopping disappointments
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building frustration. Good economy with rapid-fire delivery.

jokes.text: Christmas trees for the girls and bloody spearheads for Bart.
jokes.setup: Marge making favorite cookies for the kids
jokes.punchline: Violent cookie shape specifically for Bart
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly suited to Bart's character. Unexpected violent imagery in domestic setting.

jokes.text: That old aluminium one was so fake. I couldn't agree more. From now on, it's plastic all the way.
jokes.setup: Marge praising their new tree over the old aluminum one
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing while advocating for equally fake plastic
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic showing Homer missing the point entirely about authenticity.

jokes.text: It's 'craptacular.'
jokes.setup: Family trying the Christmas lights
jokes.punchline: Bart's portmanteau combining 'crap' and 'spectacular'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that became a cultural reference. Perfect for Bart's irreverent character.

jokes.text: Mucous 'pukeous.'
jokes.setup: Bart doing magic tricks with 'Hocus-pocus'
jokes.punchline: Gross-out rhyming variation
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart gross-out humor. Less clever than other wordplay moments.

jokes.text: The Indians used to drink water to wake up early for their attacks.
jokes.setup: Bart thinking he invented drinking water to wake up early
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact historical correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's know-it-all nature deflating Bart's pride.

jokes.text: It's always about the Indians, isn't it, Lise?
jokes.setup: Lisa's historical correction about Native Americans
jokes.punchline: Bart's exasperated meta-commentary
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Lisa's tendency to bring up social issues. Well-integrated character comedy.

jokes.text: Dear Santa, if you bring me lots of good stuff... I promise not to do anything bad... between now and when I wake up, amen. Ow! I'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Bart's prayer promising to be good
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatens violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bart's inability to maintain good behavior even during prayer.

jokes.text: Give me a 'P'! 'P'! Go! Go! Go! 'P'! 'P'! 'P'!
jokes.setup: Homer needs to use the bathroom urgently
jokes.punchline: Cheering himself on like a cheerleader while urinating
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual gag perfectly suited to Homer's childish enthusiasm for bodily functions.

jokes.text: Worked like a charm. [Groaning] [Toilet Flushing]
jokes.setup: Bart's water drinking scheme to wake up early
jokes.punchline: The inevitable biological consequence
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup - predictable but necessary for plot advancement

jokes.text: Oh, Aunt Selma. Always good for a fin.
jokes.setup: Bart opening Christmas presents early
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of predictable gift amount
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Bart's familiarity with family gift patterns.

jokes.text: Oh, I knew Santa wouldn't have time to check that list twice.
jokes.setup: Bart finding an impressive toy
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he doesn't deserve it based on his behavior
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing Bart knows he's been naughty but doesn't care.

jokes.text: Who designed this house?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to escape house fire he started
jokes.punchline: Critiquing architecture while in mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities in crisis situation. Good economy and unexpected response to danger.

jokes.text: Snow covers all. Pure, white snow.
jokes.setup: Bart looking at the aftermath of the fire
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of snow covering his crime
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between poetic language and criminal context.

jokes.text: Well, he had a glass eye, a wooden leg... a big scar on his cheek. Anything unusual? Hooks for hands, uh- Oh, he was wearing a striped convict's shirt. He was carrying a big sack with a dollar sign on it.
jokes.setup: Police asking Bart to describe the fictional burglar
jokes.punchline: Increasingly cartoonish criminal description
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd criminal stereotypes. Great structure building to comic climax.

jokes.text: Classic burglar.
jokes.setup: Bart's ridiculous burglar description
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's matter-of-fact acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery showing Wiggum's incompetence. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: God hates us.
jokes.setup: Family dealing with apparent Christmas theft
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt theological conclusion
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect economy. Very characteristic of Homer's simplistic worldview.

jokes.text: The birth of Santa.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the true meaning of Christmas
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing Santa with Jesus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his religious confusion and materialistic focus.

jokes.text: But they'll have to be pretty damn miserable to make me happy.
jokes.setup: Plan to visit nursing home to cheer themselves up
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission about deriving joy from others' misery
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Homer's selfishness in brutally honest terms.

jokes.text: Come on, kids. Pretend we got new cross-country skis.
jokes.setup: Seeing Flanders family enjoying Christmas
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to fake having fun
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's desperate competitiveness with Flanders.

jokes.text: That's not as fun as it looks. Nothing could be as fun as that looks.
jokes.setup: Family's pathetic ski pantomime
jokes.punchline: Bart's acknowledgment of how good the Flanders look
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and honest acknowledgment of their situation.

jokes.text: I can't feel nothing below my chin.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining Christmas cheer injection
jokes.punchline: Disturbing side effect delivered cheerfully
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical side effects. Good character moment for Grampa's obliviousness.

jokes.text: You know what I blame this on the breakdown of? Society. Yeah. You're right, Moe. You're always Moe.
jokes.setup: Standard discussion about societal problems
jokes.punchline: Homer's drunken pun on Moe's name
jokes.explanation: Decent pun showing Homer's drunken state and affection for Moe.

jokes.text: You take that back, Barney. No, he's right, Homer. Stay out of this, old man.
jokes.setup: Barney pointing out Homer's house on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused defensive reactions
jokes.explanation: Minor character comedy showing Homer's confusion and defensiveness.

jokes.text: Aw, that's my girl. I love you, Marjorie. Yeah, she's quite a gal. You shut up.
jokes.setup: Marge being interviewed on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer's possessive reaction to Barney's comment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's jealousy and affection for Marge.

jokes.text: no Cajun sausage for little Homer.
jokes.setup: Reporter listing tragic Christmas losses
jokes.punchline: Including Homer's adult food preference as if he's a child
jokes.explanation: Funny characterization of Homer as essentially childlike in his food desires

jokes.text: I hope you'll all choke just a little bit.
jokes.setup: News reporter's emotional appeal about the family's loss
jokes.punchline: Measured yet vindictive holiday wish
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect understatement. The 'little bit' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Aw, Marge, I don't wanna hear any more about that silver polish.
jokes.setup: Marge excited about a miracle
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes she's talking about cleaning products
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's domestic obliviousness and past experiences.

jokes.text: Well, we got a 30-footer, so you can have the top of ours.
jokes.setup: Offering to help with Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of impossibly tall tree
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about Christmas excess in Springfield.

jokes.text: Please, I don't deserve this.
jokes.setup: Orphans giving Bart their dollar
jokes.punchline: Bart's rare moment of genuine guilt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's conscience emerging in extreme circumstances.

jokes.text: 15,000 Missoulians? Holy 'schlamola'!
jokes.setup: Learning about the amount of money received
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up exclamation combining languages
jokes.explanation: Good invented exclamation showing Homer's excitement and linguistic creativity.

jokes.text: What are you gonna do with all that 'kablingy'?
jokes.setup: Discussion about the money
jokes.punchline: Moe's slang term for money
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment with made-up slang typical of Moe's speech patterns.

jokes.text: I'd kiss you, but Doctor says I'm sick.
jokes.setup: Grateful citizen wanting to thank Bart
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of contagious illness
jokes.explanation: Good economy in delivering dark humor about illness at Christmas.

jokes.text: What a deal. I'd be a sucker not to get it.
jokes.setup: Car salesman adding expensive undercoating
jokes.punchline: Homer falling for obvious upselling
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer gullibility moment. Good character integration but predictable setup.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. I jabbed you with my pen. Ow! You're still doing it. Yeah, I know.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to suggest charity instead of car purchase
jokes.punchline: Salesman deliberately continuing to stab her with pen
jokes.explanation: Escalating physical comedy showing salesman's determination to prevent ethical choice

jokes.text: Oh, will this Christmas never end?
jokes.setup: Family still dealing with Christmas chaos
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperation with the holiday
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's maturity and frustration with family chaos.

jokes.text: Lisa? Bail out!
jokes.setup: Family giving conflicting driving advice during crisis
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical suggestion to abandon ship
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character moment showing Lisa's pragmatism in crisis.

jokes.text: Well... at least the burglar's having a merry Christmas.
jokes.setup: Family car destroyed, situation hopeless
jokes.punchline: Homer finding silver lining in fictional criminal's success
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment showing Homer's ability to find absurd optimism.

jokes.text: Your hands are too weak.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to strangle Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's helpful critique of her technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy. Homer's helpful tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But one thing's for sure, you do exist.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing fictional burglar on TV
jokes.punchline: Insisting on burglar's existence despite knowing the truth
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Homer's commitment to maintaining the fiction.

jokes.text: Strong words. Strong, bewildering words.
jokes.setup: Homer's confusing statement to the burglar
jokes.punchline: Reporter's professional attempt to make sense of nonsense
jokes.explanation: Excellent reaction beat showing professional news anchor trying to process Homer's illogic.

jokes.text: I destroyed the presents. Then I buried the evidence. And I acted alone.
jokes.setup: Bart confessing to the crime
jokes.punchline: Speaking like a professional criminal or political figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation using serious crime/political confession language for childhood mishap.

jokes.text: Yeah, Jesus must be spinning in his grave.
jokes.setup: Townspeople outraged at Christmas scam
jokes.punchline: Theological impossibility stated as fact
jokes.explanation: Absurdist religious confusion - applying 'spinning in grave' to someone who allegedly resurrected

jokes.text: Yeah, Jesus must be spinning in his grave.
jokes.setup: Crowd outraged about Christmas scam
jokes.punchline: Mixing resurrection belief with grave spinning metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent religious humor mixing Christian theology with common expression.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Shoo, you lousy freeloaders. Come back when you get some parents.
jokes.setup: Angry townspeople including orphans confronting Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Moe cruelly telling orphans to get parents
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel Moe moment - telling orphans to acquire the one thing they can't have

jokes.text: In my long career, I've seen some pretty shabby things... but this putrid fraud 'out-stinks' them all.
jokes.setup: Reporter's moral outrage building
jokes.punchline: Mixing metaphors about smell and moral corruption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of reporter's outrage with mixed metaphor wordplay.

jokes.text: I think they're running out of tomatoes. Yeah, but they still got plenty of pumpkins.
jokes.setup: Family being pelted with produce by angry mob
jokes.punchline: Casual inventory of projectiles
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor treating violent situation with casual analysis.

jokes.text: Relax, Marge. This will all blow over.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing shame while being attacked
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced optimism during crisis
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer obliviousness moment. Good character integration but predictable.

jokes.text: Whatever.
jokes.setup: Someone pointing out the hated Simpson family
jokes.punchline: Teenager's apathetic response to local drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy showing generational apathy. Single word captures whole character type.

jokes.text: A family could get used to being shunned, huh?
jokes.setup: Family being treated as pariahs, getting front of line at store
jokes.punchline: Homer finding silver lining in social ostracism
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer finding unexpected benefits in terrible situation - optimistic selfishness

jokes.text: Now turn your desk around and stop learning.
jokes.setup: Teacher refusing to teach Lisa until money returned
jokes.punchline: Commanding student to actively avoid education
jokes.explanation: Absurdist punishment - teacher commanding cessation of learning as revenge

jokes.text: All the way.
jokes.setup: Teacher making Lisa turn desk around
jokes.punchline: Petty escalation of punishment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing petty adult authority escalating punishment.

jokes.text: Then while we're gone, you take our snacks. Just like you took our money.
jokes.setup: Homer warning of nuclear plant emergency
jokes.punchline: Coworkers assuming it's another Simpson scam for food theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of mistrust - assuming emergency warning is elaborate snack theft scheme

jokes.text: 'You'll all get yours in hell... you lying, thieving' blanking blankers- 'Sincerely, Moe.'
jokes.setup: Reading hate mail from townspeople
jokes.punchline: Moe's formal letter format for profanity-laden threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between formal letter structure and angry content. Perfect for Moe's character.

jokes.text: Aren't we forgetting something, Marge? You were down $5,200 dollars. But Mr. Trebek- I asked you before the game... if you knew the rules, and you said you did.
jokes.setup: Family trying to win money on Jeopardy
jokes.punchline: Marge losing more money instead of winning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of family's bad luck. Trebek's condescending tone adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I asked you before the game... if you knew the rules, and you said you did. Judges. Run, Mom.
jokes.setup: Marge loses money on a game show
jokes.punchline: Trebek's cruel enforcement and Bart's panic
jokes.explanation: Parodying Trebek's strict rule enforcement taken to threatening extremes

jokes.text: She ain't gettin' the home version.
jokes.setup: Marge's embarrassing Jeopardy performance
jokes.punchline: Audience member's blunt assessment of consolation prize denial
jokes.explanation: Good economy and escalation of Marge's humiliation with audience mockery.

jokes.text: Consider your debt repaid, neighbor. And then some.
jokes.setup: Neighbors taking all of Simpson family's possessions
jokes.punchline: Ned's cheerful accounting of their theft
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ned's passive-aggressive satisfaction with 'justice.'

jokes.text: But we would've had each other anyway. Yeah, plus lots of other stuff.
jokes.setup: Marge's attempt to find meaning in their loss
jokes.punchline: Kids' practical assessment of what they actually lost
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of sentimental moment with practical reality check from the kids.

jokes.text: Besides, it's mine. Yoink!
jokes.setup: Family fighting over last remaining washcloth
jokes.punchline: Marge joining the petty fight with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge abandoning moral high ground for material possession.
